doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: partial
reason: The document cites a real publication (Page et al., BMJ 2021;372:n71) and includes some accurate metadata, but the "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" sections are fabricated content not found in the actual PRIS
response_turn1:
LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 78
REASON: The document references a genuine publication (Page et al., BMJ 2021, doi:10.1136/bmj.n71) and correctly identifies PRISMA 2020 as a 27-item checklist replacing PRISMA 2009, but the substantive sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" are not part of the actual PRISMA 2020 statement and appear to be fabricated additions grafted onto authentic bibliographic details.
